name: AMI Labs
summary: Yann LeCun's AI startup building 'world models' - AI that learns from reality, not just language. Raised $1.03B seed at $3.5B pre-money valuation.

name: Legora
summary: AI platform for lawyers, now used by 800 law firms. Valuation tripled from $1.8B to $5.55B in one year. Grew from 40 to 400 employees.

name: Translucent
summary: AI-powered healthcare finance platform. $27M Series A led by GV (Google Ventures).

name: Nscale
summary: Largest European AI infrastructure funding ever. $2B Series C at $14.6B valuation. Major Microsoft partnership for 200K GPUs.

name: Quince
summary: AI-powered retail platform hitting $1B revenue. $500M Series E at $10.1B valuation.

name: Sage
summary: AI-powered senior care platform. $65M Series C led by Goldman Sachs Growth Equity.

name: Sandbar
summary: AI note-taking ring startup. Former Meta employees Mina Fahmi and Kirak Hong built Stream ring - voice note-taking wearable with privacy-focused design. Preorders sold out, users averaging 50+ interactions/day.

name: Mind Robotics
summary: Rivian spin-out building AI-powered industrial robots for factory floor deployment at scale. $500M Series A at $2B valuation. Total funding $615M since founding November 2025.

name: Wonderful
summary: Enterprise AI agent platform founded in 2025 by Bar Winkler (CEO) and Roey Lalazar (CTO). $150M Series B at $2B valuation. 350 employees scaling to 900. Traction: 70% enterprise expansion within 3 months, 60% reduction in handling times, 80%+ containment rates.

name: Gestala
summary: Chinese BCI startup developing non-invasive ultrasound-based brain-computer interfaces. $21.6M raised just 2 months after launch at $100M-$200M valuation. First ultrasound BCI company in China. Lead program: chronic pain management. Also researching depression, PTSD, autism, stroke rehab.

name: RunSybil
summary: AI cybersecurity startup using AI agents to automatically hack company software to find security weaknesses. Co-founded by Ari Herbert-Voss (OpenAI's first security hire) and Vlad Ionescu (former Meta red team lead). AI agent Sybil conducts continuous autonomous penetration tests against live applications. Works with Cursor, Turbopuffer, Notion, Baseten, Thinking Machines Lab, plus major financial institutions and Fortune 500 companies.

name: Andromeda AI
summary: Startup helping companies rent AI infrastructure (GPUs) on-demand without long-term contracts. Platform aggregates unused GPUs from data centers of internet providers, crypto miners, etc. 100+ provider network. Annualized revenue grew from $50M (2024) to $100M (2025). Profitable since launch. Processed 1,000+ GPU transactions since launching ~2 years ago. Customers include notable AI startups and labs spending $250M-$500M/year on infrastructure.

name: Edra
summary: NYC-based automation platform that turns existing operational data (emails, logs, support tickets, chat histories) into a living knowledge base. Founded by two former Palantir engineers - Eugen Alpeza (built out major commercial accounts, launched Palantir's AI platform) and Yannis Karamanlakis (first forward deployed AI engineer at Palantir). Building 'Living Playbooks' that learn from real workflows. Customers include HubSpot, ASOS, and Cushman & Wakefield. Total funding: $30M Series A.

name: Parallel
summary: Paris-based startup developing AI agents for hospitals. Founded in 2024 by Paul Lafforgue (CEO) and co-founders. AI agents operate directly within existing hospital software as an AI layer that learns to use software like a human - enabling deployment in weeks. Initial focus on medical coding (translating clinical data into standardized codes for reimbursement). Already deployed across multiple public and private hospitals in Europe. Previously raised $3.5M seed in April 2025. Total funding: $20M Series A.

name: BusRight
summary: All-in-one student transportation technology platform. Uses AI to ensure school buses get students safely to class on time. Platform brings driver navigation, routing, student ridership visibility, parent communication, and live GPS tracking into one unified platform. 100%+ revenue growth for 5 consecutive years, serving 36 states. Reduced call volume by 80%+ in some districts.

name: Sequen
summary: AI startup bringing TikTok-style personalization to enterprise. Large Event Models (LEMs) learn from live user actions within a session - not static profiles or third-party cookies. Privacy-forward. Sub-20-millisecond latency. Processed 10 billion monthly requests in under 18 months. 5 Fortune 500 customers. 7% revenue lift for furniture company (vs 0.4% typical), 20% net revenue lift for Fetch Rewards in 11 days.

name: XBOW
summary: Seattle-based startup building an AI-driven platform that continuously emulates attackers at machine speed to uncover deep vulnerabilities. Platform probes software releases and environments to find security weaknesses that manual pen-testing might miss. The $120M Series C values the company at over $1 billion, making it one of the few cybersecurity unicorns to emerge in 2026.

name: Halcyon
summary: AI platform that ingests and normalizes U.S. energy regulatory data from all 50 states, every ISO/RTO grid operator, and FERC dockets. Enables utilities, data center operators, and developers to query and monitor energy information in natural language. In the U.S. alone, up to $850 billion will be invested in power and digital infrastructure in 2026, creating massive demand for energy project intelligence.

name: Cloaked
summary: Consumer-first privacy and security platform offering bundled solutions including identity creation, data removal, identity theft insurance, VPN, dark web monitoring, and AI-powered call screening. Founded by brothers Arjun and Abhijay Bhatnagar in 2020. Saw 10x growth last year with 350,000+ paying customers. Has protected 10 million identities and cleaned up 1 billion+ records from data broker sites. Now expanding to enterprise.

name: Standard Template Labs (STLabs)
summary: AI-first service management platform that resolves IT requests end-to-end, not just tracks them. Founded by Amit Agarwal (ex-President/CPO of Datadog). Platform uses AI to interpret requests, orchestrate workflows, and resolve them in context using a continuously updated digital twin of the enterprise. First company incubated by ICONIQ. Seed round co-led by ICONIQ and CRV.

name: Gimlet Labs
summary: Created first 'multi-silicon inference cloud' - software allowing AI workloads to run across diverse hardware simultaneously. Claims 3x-10x inference speedup for same cost. Partners with NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, ARM, Cerebras, d-Matrix. Eight-figure revenues out of October 2025 launch; customer base doubled in 4 months. Apps currently using hardware only 15-30% of the time. Total funding: $92M.

name: Advanced Navigation
summary: Global leader in navigation and autonomous systems. Builds resilient navigation systems that work beyond GPS for sea, land, air, space. AdNav Intelligence fusion engine. 100,000+ systems deployed globally; 80%+ revenue from US and Europe; triple-digit growth. Clients include Anduril, NOAA, Hanwha, BHP, Rheinmetall, Intuitive Machines. Founded 2012 from University of Western Australia by Chris Shaw and Xavier Orr.

name: RoboForce
summary: Full-stack Physical AI robotics company deploying TITAN platform for demanding industrial jobs (solar, data centers, shipping, mining, manufacturing). Addresses critical labor shortages - 53 gigawatts of US solar delayed in 2024. 11,000+ robot orders via LOI. Spotlighted by Jensen Huang at NVIDIA GTC 2025. TITAN robot with 1mm accuracy. Team from Carnegie Mellon, Amazon Robotics, Google, Waymo, Cruise, Tesla Robotics, ABB, Apple. Deep NVIDIA collaboration.

name: PointOne
summary: AI-powered timekeeping platform for law firms. Passively tracks lawyer's computer activity to auto-complete timesheets. Grew revenue 10x since July 2025. Signed dozens of law firm customers from global 1,200-lawyer firms to solo practitioners. Total funding: $20M.

name: Interloom
summary: Munich startup solving AI 'tacit knowledge' problem for enterprises. Building 'context graph' - a living map of how problems actually get resolved within organizations. Reduced documented vs. actual operational knowledge gap from 50% to 5% at Commerzbank. Won Zurich Insurance company-wide AI competition (beating 2,000 AI-native startups).

name: Kandou AI
summary: Swiss chip interconnect startup developing copper-based alternatives to fiber optics for AI clusters. Key product Glasswing links multi-die processor components using proprietary CNRZ-5 Chord technology. Valued at $400M. Part of wave of interconnect startups raising in 2026.

name: Harvey
summary: Legal AI company offering AI tools for legal and professional services - contract analysis, compliance, due diligence, and litigation. $190M ARR (January 2026), up from $100M in August 2025. 100,000+ lawyers across 1,300 organizations. Clients include global law firms, NBCUniversal, and HSBC. Valuation $11B — one of the few AI unicorns of 2026. Total funding raised $1B+. Co-founders Winston Weinberg (CEO) and Gabe Pereyra. Plans to expand AI agents and embedded legal engineering teams globally.

name: Adonis
summary: AI orchestration platform for healthcare revenue cycle management - automates denial prevention, claim resolution, and revenue recovery for hospitals, health systems, and physician groups. Founded by brothers Akash Magoon and Aman Magoon in 2022. $40M Series C led by Quadrille Capital. Total funding: $95M. Growing demand as health systems fight AI-powered insurer denials.

name: Origin
summary: AI-powered benefits management platform. Ingest and structure fragmented benefits data across policies, contracts, renewals, vendor platforms in dozens of languages. One client (CFO estimated $750M benefits spend) expects to save $75M. Another consolidated 13 local insurance policies into one regional plan achieving 20% cost savings. Anchor customers: Pfizer, Comcast, BP. Founded by Chris Bruce and Pete Craghill (previously founded Darwin, acquired by Mercer in 2016). Value-based pricing model.

name: Cents
sector: Enterprise Software / Vertical SaaS / Laundry Industry
summary: All-in-one platform for laundromat management with point-of-sale, on-machine payments, marketing automation, AI customer service, pickup & delivery services, and live GPS tracking. 4,500+ laundromat locations processing $1B in payments annually. Largest single software investment in the laundry vertical. Serves laundromats, dry cleaners, and multi-family operations.

name: Shield AI
summary: Defense AI startup building autonomous drone systems. Nova drone became first AI-powered drone deployed in US military history. Raised $2B Series G at $12.7B valuation (up 140%). Selected by US Air Force as mission autonomy provider for CCA. Pilots 26 classes of vehicles including F-16s, jet-powered UAVs, helicopters, drone boats, and ground vehicles. Acquired Aechelon Technology (flight simulation). Gary Steele appointed as new CEO Dec 2025.

name: Granola
summary: AI meeting notes startup expanding from prosumer to enterprise. Co-founders Chris Pedregal (previously founded Socratic, acquired by Google) and Sam Stephenson. Total funding $192M. Customers include Vanta, Gusto, Thumbtack, Asana, Cursor, Lovable, Decagon, Mistral AI. Launched Spaces (team workspaces) and enterprise APIs. Valuation jumped 6x from $250M to $1.5B in under a year.

name: Lace (Lace Lithography)
summary: Norwegian startup developing helium atom beam lithography for next-gen chip manufacturing. Uses 0.1nm beam width (135x narrower than ASML's EUV light) to enable atomic-scale chip features. Could extend Moore's Law beyond current limitations. Founded July 2023 by physicist Bodil Holst and former PhD student Adria Salvador Palau. Raised $40M Series A led by Atomico with Microsoft M12 participation.

name: Blossom Health
summary: AI copilot for psychiatry. Platform augments clinicians' clinical decisions and automates back office tasks. Turns episodic care into continuous AI-driven relationship. John Zhao (CEO, ex-Athelas, ex-EverQuote) leading. Hundreds of clinicians treating 10,000+ patients across multiple states. In-network with major insurers, copays ~$22.

name: Arinna
summary: Building ultrathin solar panels from transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs) for spacecraft. 32% more efficient, flexible, cheaper, more durable than legacy space solar. Lasts 15 years on orbit. Founded by Stanford PhDs Koosha Nazif (CEO) and Alex Shearer (CTO). Testing on orbit before year-end, mass production planned 2028.

name: ORAN Development (ODC)
summary: AI-Native Radio Access Network (AI-RAN) pioneer transforming cell towers into distributed compute hubs. $45M Series A from Nvidia, Booz Allen, Cisco, Nokia, AT&T, MTN, Telecom Italia. Architect of U.S.-based Odyssey platform. Turning 5G networks into AI compute fabric for Agentic AI and Physical AI applications.

name: Deccan AI
sector: AI / GenAI Data / Foundation Model Infrastructure
summary: GenAI data company running post-training and production layer for AI labs and enterprises. Runs expert human trajectories, rigorous evals, and RL environments. Works with top frontier labs and majority of Magnificent 7 companies. Global pool of 1M+ domain experts. Operational backbone in Hyderabad. Mission: 'super accurate AI' for high-stakes business logic.

name: Glimpse
sector: AI / CPG & Retail Operations / Dispute-Tracking Fintech
summary: AI platform for CPG/retail that automates financial deduction processes and dispute tracking. Helps brands recover invalid deductions from retailers. AI agents log into retailer portals, centralize documents, classify deductions, validate against supply chain/promotion data, and automatically file disputes. Works with 200+ brands including Suave and Chapstick. System gets smarter with each deduction processed (compounding data advantage). Total funding: $52M.

name: Doctronic
sector: Healthcare AI / Digital Health / AI Doctor
summary: AI doctor platform using a multi-agent system for 24/7 autonomous triage through treatment. Chatbot consults patients then transfers to licensed physicians via telehealth ($39 fee). 15M+ AI consultations, 1M+ patients globally. Study: 81% diagnosis agreement with physicians, 99.2% treatment plan agreement, zero hallucinations. Founded 2023 by Matt Pavelle (ex-CTO Moda Operandi) and Dr. Adam Oskowitz (UCSF vascular surgeon). Expanding into academic medical centers, payers, and digital health platforms.

name: Lucid Bots
summary: Full-stack robotics company manufacturing Sherpa drones (window cleaning) and Lavo robot in the US. Charlotte-based. Approaching 1,000 units sold (grew from 100 units in 5 years). Expanding from window cleaning to painting, waterproofing, and sealing. 50+ inbound leads/month for painting before marketing. Founded 2018 by Andrew Ashur (economics/liberal arts grad) who got the idea watching window washers in danger. Anti-hype approach — selling real job site performance, not demos.

name: Swish
summary: Bengaluru-based full-stack 10-minute food delivery startup. Owns kitchens, supply chain, and delivery network — dense hyperlocal clusters within 1km radius. ~20,000 orders/day across 10 micro-markets in Bengaluru, up from 5,000 four months ago. Older kitchen clusters already profitable. Cofounders: Aniket Shah (CEO), Ujjwal Sukheja, Saran S. $38M Series B led by Hara Global and Bain Capital Ventures. Valued at $139M post-money (more than 2x valuation from a year ago). Total funding: $54M.

name: Doss
summary: AI-native inventory management layer that integrates with existing accounting systems and ERPs (Rillet, Campfire, QuickBooks). Solves the problem of keeping physical goods data synced with accounting ledgers. Targets mid-market consumer brands generating $20M-$250M revenue. Cofounder/CEO Wiley Jones pivoted from core accounting product to partner with AI-native ERP companies. Competitors: NetSuite, Didero. $55M Series B co-led by Madrona and Premji Invest, with Intuit Ventures, Theory Ventures, General Catalyst participating.

name: Saronic
summary: Austin-based defense tech startup building autonomous surface vessels (ASVs) for the U.S. military. Makes 6 vessel iterations including Spyglass (6-foot), Cutlass, Corsair (24-foot), and Marauder (180-foot, 40-metric-ton). Signed $392M Navy contract. $1.75B Series D led by Kleiner Perkins at $9.25B valuation — up from $4B last year. New investors: Advent International and Bessemer Venture Partners. Building Port Alpha facility 10x larger than Franklin, Louisiana shipyard. Plans 20+ ships/year by 2027. Ranked 19th on CNBC Disruptor 50.

name: Rebellions
summary: Seoul-based fabless AI chip startup developing inference-optimized semiconductors. Co-founded by Sunghyun Park (CEO, MIT EECS, ex-Morgan Stanley quant, Intel, SpaceX) and Jinwook Oh (CTO, KAIST, ex-IBM Research). ATOM series launched 2023 for data centers. New products: RebelRack and RebelPOD. $400M pre-IPO round at $2.34B valuation led by Mirae Asset Financial Group and Korea National Growth Fund. Total raised: $850M ($650M in last 6 months). IPO planned for later 2026. Aggressively expanding to U.S., Japan, Saudi Arabia, Taiwan.

name: Midas
summary: German platform for composable onchain investment products. Allows strategy managers to turn institutional strategies into regulatory-compliant tokens offering full transparency, instant liquidity, and native composability across DeFi protocols. Has powered over $1.7B in asset issuance across 15+ investment products, with 20,000+ mToken holders. Co-founders: Dennis Dinkelmeyer (CEO, ex-Goldman Sachs), Fabrice Grriba (ex-FJ Labs), Romain Bourgois (ex-Ondo Finance).

name: Avo
summary: EHR-integrated clinical AI platform that synthesizes patient data, care guidelines, and payer policies to guide clinicians through documentation, diagnosis, and care management. Founded by Dr. Yair Saperstein, Dr. Joongheum Park, and Laurence Coman in 2018. Total funding: $18M equity.

name: Alcatraz
summary: AI-powered face recognition access control for buildings. Founded by ex-Apple Face ID engineer Vince Gaydarzhiev. The only biometric system that authenticates WITHOUT storing faces — uses privacy-first facial authentication vs. surveillance. Customers include top AI data centers, major U.S. airports, Fortune 100 companies. 300%+ YoY growth in data center adoption. $50M Series B led by BlackPeak Capital, Cogito Capital, Taiwania Capital.

name: Sift
summary: Building the missing data layer for Physical AI. Platform transforms raw sensor and telemetry data from satellites, drones, and autonomous vehicles into structured, queryable information AI models can interpret. $42M Series B led by StepStone with GV, Riot Ventures, Fika Ventures participation. Total funding $67M.

name: Miravoice
summary: AI voice agent conducts long-form phone surveys (100+ questions, 40+ minutes) autonomously. Usage-based billing model. Surpassed 100,000 calls in 2025. 10-20 customers in production/pilot including survey orgs, market research firms, universities. $6.3M seed led by Unusual Ventures, with angels from Ramp, PubMatic, Atlassian, Google.

name: Sarvam AI
summary: India's full-stack sovereign AI platform building for all from India. Founded by AI4Bharat (IIT Madras) veterans Dr. Vivek Raghavan and Dr. Pratyush Kumar. Open-sourced Sarvam 2B (first 2B-param Indic LLM on domestic compute, 4T tokens), Sarvam 30B, and Sarvam 105B (MoE). Raised $300-350M at $1.5-1.55B valuation in round led by Bessemer with Nvidia, Amazon, Prosperity7. 2.5 years old. Positioned to bring AI to a billion Indians.

name: Moonbounce
summary: AI control engine that ensures AI systems behave exactly as designed at any scale. Converts content moderation policies into executable, updatable LLM-powered logic with sub-300ms enforcement. 40M+ daily reviews, 100M+ daily active users. Founded by ex-Facebook trust & safety lead Brett Levenson and ex-Apple cloud/AI infrastructure engineer Ash Bhardwaj. 12-person team. Serves AI companions, image generators, dating apps.

name: Reju
summary: French textile-to-textile regeneration company spun out from Technip Energies JV with IBM and Under Armour. Awarded €135M Dutch NIKI grant to build industrial-scale regeneration hub at Chemelot Industrial Park, Netherlands. Creates Reju Polyester from 100% textile waste at 50% lower carbon emissions vs virgin polyester. CEO Patrik Frisk. Partnership with Circle-8 Textile Ecosystems (UK).

name: WHOOP
summary: Human performance and health tracking wearable company. $575M Series G at $10.1B valuation — nearly 3x its $3.6B previous valuation. Led by Collaborative Fund with participation from Abbott, Mayo Clinic, Qatar Investment Authority, Mubadala, IVP, Foundry, and celebrity investors including Cristiano Ronaldo, LeBron James, Rory McIlroy. Exited 2025 at $1.1B bookings run rate, up 103% YoY. ~$900M total raised since founding in 2012. IPO rumored.

name: Starcloud
sector: Space Infrastructure / Orbital AI Data Centers
summary: Building solar-powered data centers in space. $170M Series A at $1.1B valuation — fastest Y Combinator startup to reach unicorn in 17 months. Led by Benchmark and EQT Ventures. First satellite (Starcloud-1) launched Nov 2025 with Nvidia H100 GPU — first terrestrial GPU in orbit; trained an AI model and ran Gemini from space. $200M total funding. Starcloud-2 launching 2026 with Nvidia Blackwell chip. Starcloud-3 (200kW spacecraft for Starship) aims to be cost-competitive with Earth-based data centers at ~$0.05/kw/hr.

name: Insight Health
summary: Clinical agent platform for healthcare targeting the $1T US healthcare administrative cost problem. Platform uses AI agent 'Lumi' to autonomously conduct patient intake, collect clinical history, and schedule procedures. Conducted first ever fully autonomous AI patient encounter in a clinical setting. $11M Series A led by Standard Capital (ex-YC Managing Partner Dalton Caldwell). Co-founded by ex-Segment/Y Combinator product leader and cardiologists from OHSU. Founded 2023. Based in New York.

name: Nomadic
summary: AV/robotics data infrastructure startup. Vision-language model platform turning footage into structured, searchable datasets. Solves 95% of fleet data sitting in archives problem. Agentic reasoning system for identifying edge cases in AV/robot training data. Customers include Zoox, Mitsubishi Electric, Natix Network, Zendar. Won Nvidia GTC pitch contest. Co-founders: Mustafa Bal (CEO) and Varun Krishnan (CTO), Harvard CS undergrads. Krishnan is international chess master ranked 1,549th globally.

name: Halter
sector: AgTech / Virtual Fencing and Cattle Management
summary: Solar-powered smart collar for cattle that creates virtual fences using audio and vibration cues. Cattle learn in approximately 3 interactions. Always-on behavioral data tracks health, fertility, illness flags. Lifts land productivity up to 20 percent, sometimes doubling output. 1 million cattle on collars across 2,000 plus farms in NZ, Australia, and 22 U.S. states. 5 generations of hardware. Founded by Craig Piggott (CEO, ex-Rocket Lab) at age 21. Total funding approximately 400M dollars. 220M Series E led by Founders Fund at 2B valuation.

name: Cognichip
summary: Physics-informed AI model that assists chip engineers in designing semiconductors. Aims to reduce chip development cost by 75 percent plus and cut timeline by more than half. Current chip design takes 3-5 years with 2 years in design phase alone. Nvidia Blackwell has 104B transistors. Uses proprietary, synthetic, and licensed training data with secure on-premise training option. Founded by Faraj Aalaei (third-time founder). Total funding 93M dollars. Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan joining board. Competitors: Synopsys, Cadence, ChipAgents (74M), Ricursive (300M).

name: Hermeus
summary: Defense aviation company building the fastest unmanned aircraft in the world. $350M Series C ($200M equity + $150M debt) at $1B valuation. Successfully flew Quarterhorse Mk 2.1 demonstrator; supersonic flight imminent. Scaling to fleet of 3 F-16 scale aircraft. Used modified Pratt & Whitney F100 engine. Targeting Mach 3 for US Department of Defense. ~300 employees. Total capital raised over $500M.

name: Aria Networks
summary: AI-native Ethernet switches for data center AI clusters. $125M Series A to launch Deep Networking platform. Sells 64-port 1.6T switches (air and liquid-cooled) and 800G/128-port switches. Custom SONiC-based OS with AI telemetry engine 10,000x more detailed than competitors. AI agents automate load balancing and congestion management. Founded 2025. Based in Palo Alto, CA.

name: E2 (Endovascular Engineering)
summary: Commercial-stage medtech company developing the Helo Thrombectomy Platform for treatment of venous thromboembolism (VTE) and pulmonary embolism. Patented dual-action mechanism integrating aspiration with mechanical clot disruption. Single-pass, large-bore performance through small-profile catheter. $80M Series C to commercialize and build commercial organization.

name: Xoople
sector: Earth Mapping / AI Infrastructure / Space Data
summary: Spanish startup developing satellite constellation to collect precise data for deep learning models. Building Earth observation system for AI with L3Harris partnership for sensors. Embedding into Microsoft and Esri platforms before having own satellite data. Goal: 'Earth's System of Record.' $130M Series B at unicorn valuation. Total raised: $225M.

name: Q-Factor
summary: Israeli neutral atom quantum computing startup founded by four leading physicists from Weizmann Institute and Technion. Goal: build quantum computer scaling to millions of qubits (current systems limited to few thousand). Architectural breakthrough identified bottlenecks in existing neutral atom systems. $24M Seed with backing from Intel Capital, NFX, TPY Capital.

name: Luminai
summary: AI-native enterprise automation platform for healthcare operations. Addresses $1T+ administrative cost problem (25% of healthcare spending). Builds intelligent orchestration layer that interprets unstructured inputs, generates reliable intelligence, coordinates end-to-end processes across access, revenue cycle, compliance. Team from Palantir, Cruise, Google, Coinbase, Epic. $38M Series B led by Peak XV Partners. Total raised: $60M.

name: Portal Space Systems
summary: Next-generation spacecraft manufacturer building rapidly maneuverable vehicles for operations across and between orbital regimes. Commercializing solar thermal propulsion technology previously validated at NASA and Air Force Research Lab. Starburst spacecraft targeting rendezvous and proximity operations demo on SpaceX Transporter-18 Q4 2026. Supernova targeting 2027 debut with 80% shared systems with Starburst. CEO Jeff Thornburg. ~40 employees scaling to 100 in 2026. Building 52,000 sq ft manufacturing facility in Bothell, WA. Named Via Satellite Top 10 Startup to Watch.

name: AfterQuery
summary: AI-powered platform capturing expert reasoning from nearly 100,000 verified professionals (doctors, lawyers, financial analysts, software engineers) to train AI models on how experts think through complex problems. Passed $100M ARR. Every major AI frontier lab is a customer. Co-founded by Spencer Mateega (CEO, ex-Google/Meta, Wharton), Carlos Georgescu (ex-Citadel/Meta, prior ed-tech exit), and Danny Tang (Wharton, prior startup sale). Y Combinator Winter 2025. Raised $30M Series A at $300M valuation led by Altos Ventures.

name: HeyDonto AI Technology
summary: Dental AI startup building Conduit, an interoperability platform enabling data exchanges between dental and medical providers, plus revenue cycle management services. Enables electronic physician-to-dentist referrals and real-time dentist-to-physician clearances. Raised $20M seed at $200M valuation from a syndicate of 8 dental industry veterans and practicing dentists. Founded by Rivers Morrell (CEO, ex-Topscale AI). Based in Chicago.

name: Firmus
summary: Asia AI data center provider building 'Project Southgate' — energy-efficient AI factory network in Australia and Tasmania using Nvidia's Vera Rubin platform. Raised $505M led by Coatue at $5.5B post-money valuation. Has raised $1.35B in six months total. Previously raised AU$330M (~$215M) at AU$1.85B valuation from Nvidia. Crypto-roots-turned-AI provider now planning ASX IPO.

name: Patlytics
summary: First end-to-end AI platform for the entire patent lifecycle — from application drafting and prosecution to infringement analysis, invalidity research, and portfolio management. Trusted by over 40% of Am Law 100 firms. Serves Quinn Emanuel, Foley & Lardner, Susman Godfrey, Canon, Rivian, Xerox, Asahi Kasei, and TaylorMade. 4.5x revenue growth in 12 months. $40M Series B led by SignalFire.

name: Haast
summary: AI-powered enterprise compliance engine that embeds organizational policy and risk frameworks directly into enterprise workflows. Solves the compliance bottleneck created by AI-driven content explosion (8x–10x content volume). Fortune 500 customers. 4.5x revenue growth in 12 months with zero customer churn. Founded 2023 by Kunal Vankadara (CEO). $12M Series A led by Peak XV Partners.

name: SiFive
summary: RISC-V chip IP company founded by the Berkeley engineers who created the open-source RISC-V instruction set architecture. $400M Series G at $3.65B valuation — described as the final round before IPO. CEO Patrick Little (former Qualcomm exec). Makes chip blueprints used by companies like Alphabet for custom AI data center processors.

name: Inertia Enterprises
summary: Commercial fusion energy company founded by Twilio co-founder Jeff Lawson (CEO), Dr. Annie Kritcher (Chief Scientist, lead NIF fusion experiment designer), and Stanford Prof. Mike Dunne (CTO, former LLNL Fusion Energy Program Director). $450M Series A — one of the largest fusion rounds ever. Pursuing inertial confinement fusion validated by NIF's December 2022 ignition breakthrough. Plans commercial fusion power plant by 2030.

name: SportIQ
sector: Sports Technology / AI Analytics / Smart Hardware
summary: Smart basketball technology company with a 1-gram FIBA-approved sensor embedded in the ball's air valve. NBA Launchpad graduate (Summer 2025). CEO Erik Anderson. Technology captures detailed movement, spin, and bounce data for analytics and automated officiating. $6.2M Series A from KB Partners, Koppenberg Management, and Match Ventures. Total funding $9.16M. Planning consumer product launch Q2 2026.

name: Slate Auto
summary: Jeff Bezos-backed EV startup building an affordable electric pickup truck targeting the mid-$20,000s price point. 160,000+ refundable reservations. Led by former Amazon Consumer CEO Jeff Wilke as co-founder and former Amazon Marketplace VP Peter Faricy as CEO. $650M Series C brings total funding to ~$1.4B. Manufacturing in Indiana.

name: Chapter
summary: AI-driven Medicare navigation platform for seniors. Surpassed $100M ARR with 3x revenue growth in 2025. Reviews every Medicare plan nationwide and pairs AI with licensed advisors for personalized, unbiased guidance. $100M Series E at $3B valuation led by Al Gore's Generation Investment Management.

name: Remission Medical
summary: Virtual rheumatology company partnering with health systems to deliver embedded specialty care via AI-native workforce platform RemissionOS. Addresses 54M+ Americans affected by rheumatic diseases with average 2+ month wait times for specialists. Triple-digit growth across national health system partners. Series A led by Blue Heron Capital.

name: Paxos Labs
summary: Spin-off from Paxos building DeFi infrastructure for enterprises. Products: Amplify — single-SDK platform for branded stablecoins, yield earning, and crypto lending. Co-founded by Bhaumik Kotecha and led by Charles Cascarilla (CEO of Paxos). On pace to break even by end of 2026. Customers include neobank Hyperbeat and developers behind Aleo blockchain.

name: nEye.ai
summary: Building OCS-on-a-chip: optical circuit switches built directly onto a chip for AI data centers. Combines silicon photonics, MEMS, and CMOS. Solves AI connectivity bottleneck — as AI systems grow, moving data between GPUs/CPUs/memory at speed has become the limiting factor. $80M Series C led by Sutter Hill Ventures. Total funding $152M. Market for Optical Circuit Switching projected to surpass $3B within 3 years.

name: Prefix
summary: AI-powered facility management platform for multi-location restaurants and retailers. $500B market — brands spend ~$500B/year maintaining spaces. Prefix acts as coordination hub between operators and local service providers (HVAC, refrigeration, plumbing, electrical). Agentic AI handles communication, scheduling, and task tracking. Nearly 2,000 locations across 50 brands (Bojangles, Chipotle, Raising Cane's, Famous Footwear, L'Occitane). $2.5M ARR, 500% YoY growth.

name: Glydways
summary: San Francisco-based startup developing personal autonomous pods on dedicated 2-meter-wide lanes. $170M Series C (in talks for $250M more at $1B+ valuation). Backed by Sam Altman (Series B investor). Vinod Khosla sits on board. Three pilots launching 2026 in Atlanta, NYC, and UAE. Can move up to 10,000 people/hour/lane at 90% lower infrastructure cost vs rail. Founded 2016 by Mark Seeger and Zachary Zeliff.

name: Factory
summary: AI agents for enterprise engineering teams. Customers include Morgan Stanley, Ernst & Young, and Palo Alto Networks. Key differentiator is ability to switch between foundation models (Claude, DeepSeek). Raised $150M at $1.5B valuation.

name: Gizmo
summary: AI-powered learning platform with 13M+ users across 120+ countries. Gamified study materials from student notes. Raised $22M Series A led by Shine Capital.

name: Parasail
summary: Cloud compute service for AI inference. Generating 500 billion tokens/day. 40 data centers in 15 countries. Focus on inference-only workloads. Raised $32M Series A.

name: Artemis
summary: Cybersecurity platform delivering real-time threat detection and automated response for AI-powered attacks. Raised $70M combined seed + Series A led by Felicis.

name: Traza
summary: AI-powered procurement platform deploying autonomous agents for vendor outreach, order tracking, and invoice processing for manufacturers and construction companies. Raised $2.1M Pre-Seed led by Base10 Partners.

name: Loop
summary: Full-stack AI platform for logistics and supply chains that predicts disruptions and provides prescriptive recommendations. Co-founders Matt McKinney (CEO) and Shaosu Liu (CTO) met at Uber. Processes fragmented operational and financial data to provide visibility, cost control, and decision-making intelligence. Positioning to become 'the intelligence layer of the entire supply chain.'

name: InsightFinder
summary: AI-driven observability platform that monitors and ensures reliability of AI models and entire tech stacks. Founded by CEO Helen Gu (NC State CS professor, ex-IBM/Google), uses unsupervised ML, LLMs, predictive AI, and causal inference to detect, diagnose, remediate, and prevent AI model failures. Customers include UBS, NBCUniversal, Lenovo, Dell, Google Cloud, Comcast. Revenue grew 3x+ in past year. Won seven-figure Fortune 50 deal in 3 months prompting this raise.

name: Hilbert AI
summary: Building AI growth agents that autonomously make commercial decisions for consumer companies: pricing, promotion timing, product assortment, and marketing spend allocation. Starts with solving the 'data plumbing' problem — structuring and labeling customer data schemas so agents can execute. Co-founders Nazli Tan (CEO, ex-Getir Head of Growth) and Ceyda Erten (CSO, ex-Getir Head of Transformation) built and scaled Getir's growth function across 9 countries. Works with major retailers and fast-growing AI-native startups.

name: Synera
summary: Agentic AI platform orchestrating the full industrial engineering value chain — described as 'JARVIS for engineers.' Deploys teams of AI agents that autonomously execute complex workflows across the product lifecycle, connecting 80+ CAx tools, data, knowledge, and processes into a unified orchestrated system. Serves NASA, Airbus, BMW, Volvo Trucks, Brose, L'Oreal, Miele, STIHL across 60+ enterprise customers in 15 countries. Doubled ARR in 2025; 60% of new business from AI offering. Founded by Dr. Moritz Maier (CEO).

name: Artemis
summary: AI-native defense system fighting AI-powered attacks at machine speed. Constantly watches log-ins, cloud activity, apps across organizations, learns 'normal' patterns, spots anomalies in real-time, connects dots into clear attack stories, and can automatically shut down attacks before they spread. Founded by former AWS product leader (Shachar Hirshberg, CEO) and former head of AI at Abnormal Security (Dan Shiebler, CTO). Positioned as next-gen alternative to Splunk for AI-driven threat environment. Several seven-figure deals closed, expecting multimillion-dollar ARR before end of 2026. Clients include Mercury, Wix, Lemonade, and Abnormal AI.

name: Gizmo
summary: AI-powered learning platform transforming students' notes into interactive study materials (quizzes, flashcards) using game mechanics. 13M+ users across 120+ countries (up from 300K in 2023). Founded by three Cambridge University alumni (Petros Christodoulou as CEO, Paul Evangelou as CPO, Robin Jack as CTO). Targets teenagers and young adults with leaderboards, streaks, limited daily lives, and friend challenges. Addressing declining US academic performance and reduced attention spans. Competing with Anki, Quizlet, Nibble, Yuno, and Knowt.

name: Pillar
summary: AI-powered financial risk management platform automating hedging for commodity-driven businesses. Ingests data from contracts, cash flows, inventories, ERP software, spreadsheets, and WhatsApp messages to continuously analyze exposure across commodities, FX, and freight. Founded by Harsha Ramesh (CEO) and Chinmay Deshpande (CTO). Clients include Shibuya Sakura Industries, Sigma Recycling, and United Metal Solutions Group.

name: Factory
summary: AI agents for enterprise engineering teams. Develops 'Droids' that autonomously execute software engineering tasks. Key differentiator: ability to switch between foundation models (Claude, DeepSeek). Revenue doubled month over month for 6 consecutive months. Customers include Morgan Stanley, Ernst & Young, and Palo Alto Networks. Founded by Matan Grinberg (CEO, ex-UC Berkeley PhD student) and Eno Reyes (CTO). Keith Rabois (Khosla Ventures) joined board.

name: Recursive Superintelligence
summary: Building AI systems that autonomously improve their own code and architecture — eliminating human intervention from the AI training process. Core thesis: 'The bottleneck for AI progress is no longer just compute or data, but human intervention.' If successful, model improvements could take 18 hours instead of 18 months. Four-month-old London-incorporated startup. Co-founded by ex-Google DeepMind and ex-OpenAI researchers Tim Rocktäschel, Josh Tobin, Jeff Clune, and Tim Shi. Plans first 'Level 1' autonomous training run later this year.

name: Reliable Robotics
summary: Autonomous aircraft systems developer. Building the Reliable Autonomy System (RAS) for fully automated aircraft operation. M funding at ~B valuation. 200+ system commitments from commercial and military customers. FAA certification in progress.

name: Ray Therapeutics
summary: Optogenetic vision restoration therapies for severe retinal degenerative diseases. Lead program RTx-021 has RMAT designation. M Series B for retinitis pigmentosa, Stargardt disease, and geographic atrophy programs. ~M total raised.

name: Cursor (Anysphere)
summary: AI-powered code editor - one of fastest-growing startups in history. B run rate as of Feb 2026, forecast to hit B by year-end. In talks for B at B+ valuation. SpaceX has option to buy for B. Co-founders all MIT students. YC graduate.

name: Project Prometheus
summary: Jeff Bezos's physical AI lab focused on real-world industrial processes — manufacturing, aerospace engineering, and semiconductor production. Co-founded with Vik Bajaj (ex-Google X, Stanford professor). Raised ~$10B at $38B valuation. Competing against OpenAI, Google DeepMind, xAI, Anthropic. Also exploring $100B fund for AI ecosystem investments.

name: Lucra
summary: B2B esports loyalty platform enabling brands to run interactive tournaments and gamified loyalty programs. Customers include Five Iron Golf, Chess Kings, and Dave & Buster's. ARK Invest's first-ever lead investment.

name: Monk
summary: AI-native accounts receivable platform automating the full contract-to-cash lifecycle for B2B companies. Customers include ElevenLabs and Profound. Manages over $1B in receivables. Delivers 40% DSO reduction, 25+ hours/month saved for AR teams.

name: Univity
summary: Paris-based startup building VLEO (very low Earth orbit) satellite constellation for 5G connectivity. Targeting 1,600+ satellites to help telecom operators extend coverage from space. Founded 2022 by Charles Delfieux. Total secured funding: €68M.

name: ComfyUI
summary: Node-based workflow platform giving creators granular control over AI image/video/audio generation from diffusion models. 4M+ users. Addresses the '60-80% there' problem where standard prompt-based AI requires 'slot machine' approach for final refinements. Used for visual effects, animation, advertising, industrial design.

name: NeoCognition
sector: AI Research Lab / Self-Learning AI Agents
summary: AI research lab developing self-learning agents that can specialize in any domain like humans. Current AI agents complete tasks only ~50% of the time. NeoCognition builds agents that mirror human rapid specialization. Founded by Ohio State professor Yu Su (Sloan Research Fellow). 15 employees, majority with PhDs.

name: Era
sector: AI Gadgets / Software Platform for AI Hardware
summary: Software platform enabling hardware makers to create AI agents and orchestrations for AI devices. Platform provides 130+ LLMs from 14+ providers for different AI gadget form factors. Replaces app layer with intelligence layer. Founders from Humane, HP, and io project.
